ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken soup' class (FOODON_03302731); no specific entry for Chinese double-boiled or steamed-vessel chicken soup exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC represents chicken soup as a category. No specific code for yuan zhong (steamed-vessel) preparation exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for steamed chicken soup or yuan zhong chicken soup found in the SNOMED CT food hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one individual steamed clay pot of chicken soup, weighing around 350 g, as typically served at Din Tai Fung and similar Chinese restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken soup, home-prepared (USDA SR Legacy Item 06172 / FoodData Central reference for clear chicken broth and chicken soup)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Yuan zhong steamed chicken soup is a clear, minimally seasoned Chinese chicken broth prepared by slow steaming whole chicken pieces in a sealed vessel for 6–8 hours. No direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry was found for this specific preparation. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for clear chicken soup / chicken broth (FDC ID 172934 and 171161), cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods chicken broth data. The preparation method (no added oil, minimal seasoning) is consistent with the low-fat, low-carbohydrate profile of a clear chicken broth. Sodium is estimated at the lower end of the range given the restaurant's premium positioning and minimal seasoning description. Values represent per-100 g of the liquid soup as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021; analogous estimate based on clear chicken broth / low-carbohydrate meat soup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for yuan zhong steamed chicken soup. The GI is estimated at 30 (low) based on analogous clear meat soups and broths in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables (Atkinson et al., 2021, Am J Clin Nutr). Clear chicken soup contains minimal carbohydrate (approximately 0.8 g per 100 g), resulting in a negligible glycemic response. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (30 × 2.8) / 100 ≈ 0.8, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving ≈ 2.8 g.

hypertension.guidance: Each 350 g serving provides approximately 735 mg of sodium, representing about 32% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients managing blood pressure should consume this soup in moderation and avoid adding soy sauce or salt at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only approximately 2.8 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of less than 1 per serving, yuan zhong steamed chicken soup has a negligible impact on postprandial blood glucose and is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as part of a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and no added oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The double-boiling preparation method uses no added oil, resulting in low total fat (approximately 2.1 g per serving) and low saturated fat (approximately 0.7 g per serving), making this soup a heart-friendly protein source when sodium intake is monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuan Zhong Ji Tang (元盅鸡汤), known in English as Steamed Chicken Soup, is a classic Chinese clear chicken soup prepared by the traditional double-boiling or steamed-vessel method. Premium whole chicken pieces are placed in a sealed ceramic or clay vessel (yuan zhong) with water, ginger, and minimal seasoning, then steamed or slow-simmered for six to eight hours. This extended, gentle cooking process extracts the natural collagen, amino acids, and minerals from the chicken bones and meat without the use of added oil or strong seasonings, producing a sparkling-clear, deeply flavourful golden broth. The soup has a clean, delicate, savoury taste with a silky mouthfeel derived from dissolved collagen and gelatin. It is a signature dish at Din Tai Fung and other premium Chinese restaurants in Singapore and Taiwan. Nutritionally, the soup is low in calories (approximately 17 kcal per 100 g), low in fat, very low in carbohydrate, and provides a modest amount of protein and bioavailable minerals including potassium, phosphorus, and zinc. Its very low glycemic load makes it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The moderate sodium content (approximately 210 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for those with hypertension. The absence of added oil and low saturated fat content make it a cardiovascular-friendly choice. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry was found for yuan zhong steamed chicken soup. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy clear chicken soup / broth entries, which are consistent with the described preparation method (no added oil, minimal seasoning, long slow steaming). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for clear meat soups. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact Singapore-specific database entry was matched.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (clear chicken soup / chicken broth, FDC IDs 172934 and 171161) as no direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for yuan zhong steamed chicken soup. Values represent the liquid soup as served. 2. Dietary fibre is 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed zero as the food is a clear strained broth with no vegetable solids. 3. Vitamin C is 0.0 mg per 100 g; this is naturally zero as the food is a clear meat broth with no fruit or vegetable components. 4. Trans fat is 0.0 g per 100 g; confirmed zero as no hydrogenated fats are used in this preparation. 5. Fields marked -1 (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) are set to -1 because these micronutrients are not reported or are unreliable in the adapted USDA SR Legacy broth entries used; direct measurement data for this specific preparation is not available. 6. GI is an analogous estimate of 30 (low) based on clear meat soups in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for yuan zhong steamed chicken soup was found. 7. Sodium is estimated at the lower end of the range for clear chicken soup given the restaurant's premium positioning and the description of minimal seasoning. 8. The food is served at Din Tai Fung Marina Bay Link Mall, Singapore, at SGD 15.79 per portion. 9. FoodOn match type is closest_parent (FOODON_03302731, chicken soup); FoodEx2 match type is closest_parent (A00LC, chicken soup); SNOMED CT match type is none as no specific concept was found.
